name: Sauteed Mixed Vegetables in Taro Nest
recipeIngredient: 40 g Soaked dried black Chinese mushrooms (approximately 5 pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut taro lengthways into thin slices (3 mm). Slice two-thirds into thin strips for the edge of the basket, and one-third into 1.5 cm wide strips for the base.
2. Marinate taro with salt for 1 hour, then rinse under cold running water for 3 hours. Drain and mix with cornstarch. Form a woven basket in one mold and place the second mold on top. Deep-fry framed taro over low flame.
3. Slice mushrooms into four, halve fungi, remove snow pea strings, cut celery diagonally, and cut carrot into patterned pieces.
4. Mix sauce ingredients: 4 tbsp water, 1 tbsp cornstarch, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp sugar.
5. For garnish (optional): Slice black mushrooms, peel tomatoes, remove seeds, and cut each into 4 slices. Cut baby corns and mustard green stems in half, drain black fungi.
6. Bring garnish seasoning ingredients to a boil, add mustard green stems and black mushrooms for 1 minute, then remaining garnish ingredients for another minute. Drain and trim.
7. Bring seasoning ingredients to a boil, add sauteed vegetables (except snow peas) and cook for 1 minute. Remove vegetables.
8. Blanch snow peas in boiling oil for 1 minute, remove when crisp, and pour excess oil out of the wok.
9. Add sauce and remaining sauteed vegetables. Saute for 1 minute.
10. Place taro basket in the middle of the serving dish and arrange trimmed vegetable garnish strips around it. Place sauteed vegetables in the basket.
